message.content:
You are doing a mechanical AsciiDoc xref repair in ONE file. Do not touch any other file.

**File**: `/Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster/Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSGS-GettingStarted.adoc`

**Background**: BBAAQ retired four SA cult-verb quoin attributes in RBS0's mapping section. Their replacement targets are already settled in RBS0. You are flipping the dangling `{retired_quoin}` attribute references in RBSGS to the live ones.

**Replacement rules** (these are the ONLY edits to make):

| Retired attribute | Replacement |
|-------------------|-------------|
| `{rbtgo_director_knight}` | `{rbtgo_director_invest}` |
| `{rbtgo_retriever_charter}` | `{rbtgo_retriever_invest}` |
| `{rbtgo_sa_forfeit}` | per call site (see below) |
| `{rbtgo_sa_list}` | per call site (see below) |

The `sa_forfeit` and `sa_list` retirements split per role: there's now `{rbtgo_director_divest}` / `{rbtgo_retriever_divest}` and `{rbtgo_director_roster}` / `{rbtgo_retriever_roster}`. You must read the surrounding prose at each call site and pick the correct variant.

**Specific sites in RBSGS** (verified via grep):

- Line 135: `{rbtgo_director_knight}` — straight flip → `{rbtgo_director_invest}`
- Line 144: `{rbtgo_retriever_charter}` — straight flip → `{rbtgo_retriever_invest}`
- Line 156: `{rbtgo_sa_list}` — context: the Governor "maintains visibility into issued credentials through {rbtgo_sa_list}" — this is GENERIC (covers both director and retriever credentials). Rewrite the prose to mention BOTH variants. Suggested replacement: `{rbtgo_director_roster} and {rbtgo_retriever_roster}`. Keep the surrounding sentence flow.
- Line 158: `{rbtgo_sa_forfeit}` — context: "can revoke access through {rbtgo_sa_forfeit}" — same generic both-roles concern. Suggested: `{rbtgo_director_divest} or {rbtgo_retriever_divest}`.
- Line 272: `{rbtgo_sa_forfeit}` — context: "the {rbtr_governor} executes {rbtgo_sa_forfeit} for the target service account, invalidating the corresponding {rbtgi_rbra_file}" — same both-roles concern. Suggested: `{rbtgo_director_divest} or {rbtgo_retriever_divest}` (or rephrase if needed for grammatical flow).

**Constraints**:
- Read the file first to confirm line numbers (the file may have been edited since the inventory grep).
- Use the Edit tool with sufficient context to make each replacement unambiguous.
- Preserve line-break and indentation patterns of the surrounding prose. AsciiDoc cares about line structure: each attribute reference often sits alone on its line.
- After all edits, run this verification grep and report the result:

```
grep -n 'rbtgo_director_knight\|rbtgo_retriever_charter\|rbtgo_sa_forfeit\|rbtgo_sa_list' /Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster/Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/RBSGS-GettingStarted.adoc
```

Expected: zero hits.

**Out of scope** — DO NOT touch:
- Any file other than RBSGS-GettingStarted.adoc
- The RBRR retirements (rbrr_depot_project_id, etc.)
- Anything in RBS0
- Behavioral or structural changes beyond the xref flips

**Report back** (under 150 words):
- Each line number you edited and the before→after replacement
- Final verification grep result
- Any surprise (e.g., a line number drifted, prose required more rewriting than expected)

content.text: I'll read the file first to confirm line numbers and surrounding context.

content.content:
120	{rbrr_governor_rbra_file}.
121	The
122	{rbtr_governor}
123	can now operate independently of the
124	{rbtr_payor}.
125	
126	== Credential Administration
127	
128	The
129	{rbtr_governor}
130	manages the roster of credentialed users throughout the
131	{rbtgi_depot}
132	lifecycle.
133	While credential creation is typically infrequent, this administrative function remains active.
134	
135	{rbtgo_director_knight}
136	provisions a
137	{rbtr_director}
138	service account, producing an
139	{rbtgi_rbra_file}
140	at the path specified by
141	{rbrr_director_rbra_file}.
142	Directors submit builds and manage images.
143	
144	{rbtgo_retriever_charter}
145	provisions a
146	{rbtr_retriever}
147	service account, producing an
148	{rbtgi_rbra_file}
149	at the path specified by
150	{rbrr_retriever_rbra_file}.
151	Retrievers pull images for local use.
152	
153	The
154	{rbtr_governor}
155	also maintains visibility into issued credentials through
156	{rbtgo_sa_list}
157	and can revoke access through
158	{rbtgo_sa_forfeit}.
159	
160	== Credential Files
161	
162	Each {rbtr_role} maintains its own credential file.
163	Understanding these files helps administrators plan secure handling.
164	
165	The
166	{rbtr_payor}
167	credentials reside in `~/.rbw/payor/rbro.env`, containing the
168	{rbro_client_secret}
169	and
170	{rbro_refresh_token}.
171	This file exists only on the administrator's
172	{at_workstation}.
173	
174	The
175	{rbtr_governor},
176	{rbtr_director},
177	and
178	{rbtr_retriever}
179	each have an
180	{rbtgi_rbra_file}
181	at paths defined in the repository configuration.
182	Each file contains
183	{giam_service_account}
184	credentials scoped to one role within one
185	{rbtgi_depot}.
186	
187	All credential files require `600` permissions, exclusion from version control, and secure distribution to authorized users.
188	A credential file cannot operate outside its designated role and {rbtgi_depot}.
189	
190	== Day-to-Day Operations
191	
192	Once setup and credential administration are complete, ongoing work proceeds through
193	{rbtr_director}
194	and
195	{rbtr_retriever}
196	roles.
197	
198	=== Director Operations
199	
200	The
201	{rbtr_director}
202	manages the build lifecycle.
203	
204	{rbtgo_ark_conjure}
205	submits a container build to
206	{gcb_service}
207	via `builds.create`.
208	The
209	{rbtr_mason}
210	executes the build within the cloud and publishes the resulting
211	{rbtgi_image}
212	to the
213	{rbtgi_repository}.
214	
215	{rbtgo_image_delete}
216	removes an
217	{rbtgi_image}
218	from the
219	{rbtgi_repository}.
220	
221	**MISSING**: {rbtgi_image} listing operation is not yet implemented.
222	
223	=== Retriever Operations
224	
225	The
226	{rbtr_retriever}
227	accesses built artifacts.
228	
229	{rbtgo_image_retrieve}
230	pulls an
231	{rbtgi_image}
232	from the
233	{rbtgi_repository}
234	to the local
235	{at_workstation}.
236	
237	{rbtgo_ark_inspect}
238	displays the trust posture of an
239	{rbtga_ark}
240	by querying GAR directly via the Registry API — no local summon required.
241	Authenticates as
242	{rbtr_retriever}.
243	Use {rbtc_plumb_compact} for a summary or {rbtc_plumb_full} for complete detail including package inventory and Dockerfile.
244	Hallmark-only invocation (single argument) navigates the hallmark subtree directly to locate the ark.
245	
246	== Recovery
247	
248	If `~/.rbw/payor/rbro.env` is lost, the administrator obtains a fresh
249	{rbtgi_json_key_file}
250	from Google Cloud Console and re-executes
251	{rbtgo_payor_install}.
252	
253	If OAuth tokens expire,
254	{rbtgo_payor_refresh}
255	obtains fresh credentials.
256	
257	If
258	{rbtr_governor}
259	credentials are compromised or need rotation, the
260	{rbtr_payor}
261	executes
262	{rbtgo_governor_mantle}
263	to replace the existing
264	{rbtr_governor}
265	service account with a fresh one, generating new
266	{rbtgi_rbra_file}
267	credentials.
268	
269	To revoke a user's access, the
270	{rbtr_governor}
271	executes
272	{rbtgo_sa_forfeit}
273	for the target service account, invalidating the corresponding
274	{rbtgi_rbra_file}.
275

input.description: Verify zero remaining retired attribute references

content.text:
Verification grep returned zero hits (exit 1 = no matches).

**Edits applied:**

- **Line 135**: `{rbtgo_director_knight}` → `{rbtgo_director_invest}`
- **Line 144**: `{rbtgo_retriever_charter}` → `{rbtgo_retriever_invest}`
- **Lines 153-158** (sentence): `... through {rbtgo_sa_list} and can revoke access through {rbtgo_sa_forfeit}.` → `... through {rbtgo_director_roster} and {rbtgo_retriever_roster}, and can revoke access through {rbtgo_director_divest} or {rbtgo_retriever_divest}.` Each attribute placed on its own line per the file's prevailing one-attribute-per-line pattern.
- **Lines 269-274** (Recovery section): `executes {rbtgo_sa_forfeit} for the target service account` → `executes {rbtgo_director_divest} or {rbtgo_retriever_divest} for the target service account`. Same one-per-line splitting.

**Verification**: `grep` for all four retired attributes returned zero hits.

**No surprises**: line numbers matched the inventory exactly; the per-line attribute formatting in RBSGS made the both-roles split natural (just two attribute lines joined by `and` / `or`).
